text: The world's most famous robot dog can now do backflips. But why? Turns out this is surprisingly important. So Spot is currently out in the field doing jobs that require climbing stairs or rough terrain or balancing or carrying very heavy loads. And so there will be times where it needs to use the full power of its motor, like if it falls while carrying something really heavy. And so what better way to test its power than to see if it can do a backflip. But it wasn't built to do that. So first, they modeled how Spot would even do a backflip in simulation. And then they rewarded it when it did it correctly. And then they finally tried it on the real robot, and it failed a lot. But after a bunch of tweaks, it finally worked. So now it can just do a backflip. For optimistic science and tech stories, follow along.
